message:
feat(vfs): auto-delete anonymous temp files on close + io_uring fault injection contract

Two VFS-layer improvements, one behavioral fix and one new test
contract for the io_uring backend.

crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/unix.rs

- UnixVfs::open(): when path is None (anonymous temp DB), compute
  delete_on_close = VfsOpenFlags::DELETEONCLOSE || is_temp instead
  of just reading the DELETEONCLOSE bit.
- Both the "already open in the registry" and the "fresh inode"
  branches of open() now pass this combined delete_on_close value
  through to the File struct.
- Rationale: SQLite's semantics for anonymous temp files are
  "delete on close" whether or not the caller explicitly passed
  VfsOpenFlags::DELETEONCLOSE.  Without this fix,
  UnixVfs would leave anonymous temp files on disk under
  /tmp/.fsqlite-* if the caller only passed TEMP_DB + CREATE +
  READWRITE, leaking files on every short-lived temp DB open.
  rusqlite / libsqlite3 do the right thing here automatically,
  so parity testing masked the leak.
- New test test_unix_vfs_anonymous_temp_file_auto_deletes_on_close
  opens a TEMP_DB, writes to it, closes it, and asserts the
  temp directory entry is gone.  This pins the invariant.

crates/fsqlite-vfs/tests/bd_3u7_4_vfs_contract_io_uring_faults.rs (new)

- Contract test for bd-3u7.4: io_uring VFS backend must survive
  injected IO faults (read EIO, write EIO, fsync ENOSPC) and
  surface them as FrankenError with enough context for the
  pager/WAL to roll back cleanly.
- Gated on feature = "native" + target_os = "linux" so it's a
  no-op on other platforms.
- Builds an IoUringVfs pointed at a tmpfile, wraps the backing
  fd in a fault-injecting shim that toggles specific errno
  returns for the N-th operation of a given kind, and walks
  through the standard pager open/read/write/fsync sequence
  asserting that:
    * read faults return FrankenError with the injected errno
      visible in the error chain;
    * write faults roll back the partial write and leave the
      file at its pre-fault state;
    * fsync faults mark the coordinator as needs-durability-
      recheck rather than silently succeeding.
- Also includes a baseline "no fault injected" control run so
  a regression in the happy path is distinguishable from a
  fault-injection-specific break.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
